metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3116
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.3: "Deletion of plexin-D1 dramatically increases mEPSC frequency in direct pathway MSNs"
Fig.4: "Deletion of plexin-D1 slightly decreases spine density in direct pathway MSNs"
Fig.6: "Postnatal deletion of plexin-D1 perturbs spine density and mEPSC frequency in direct but not indirect pathway MSNs"
Fig.7: "Loss of plexin-D1 in direct pathway MSNs selectively increases the strength of thalamostriatal glutamatergic inputs"

Abstract: "Here we show that the interaction between the secreted semaphorin 3E (Sema3E) and its receptor Plexin-D1 is a critical determinant of synaptic specificity in cortico-thalamo-striatal circuits in mice. We find that sema3E is highly expressed in thalamostriatal projection neurons whereas, in the striatum, plexin-D1 is selectively expressed in direct pathway medium spiny neurons (MSNs). Despite physical intermingling of the MSNs, genetic ablation of plexin-D1 or sema3E results in functional and anatomical rearrangement of thalamostriatal synapses specifically in direct pathway MSNs without effects on corticostriatal synapses. Thus, our results demonstrate that Sema3E and Plexin-D1 specify the degree of glutamatergic connectivity between a specific source and target within the complex circuitry of the basal ganglia."

Note:
- Complexin-D1 and Sema3A together create a repulsive cue that repel neurons from making a synapse.
- Because these proteins represent a repulsive cue that causes these neurons NOT to form a synapse, I have refrained from making the annotation to term 'regulation of synapse assembly'. Experimental description: - "plexin-D1flox/flox (plexin-D1f/f) mice41 and sema3E knock out (sema3E-/-) mice..."
